_comment: Top-100 framework registry. Each entry: id, language, category, matcher patterns (regex), tier (1=hand-curated retriever in retrievers/, 2=light pattern block in this file, 3=stub). Add new frameworks by dropping an entry here; promote to tier-1 by writing retrievers/<id>.ts. The community is encouraged to expand both depth (tier-3 → tier-2 → tier-1) and breadth.

frameworks.note: Vue 3 Composition API: setup script, <script setup>, defineProps, defineEmits, ref/reactive, computed, watch/watchEffect, provide/inject. Pinia replaces Vuex.
Nuxt 3: file-based routing in pages/, server routes in server/api/, useState/useFetch composables, layers via extends, Nitro engine for deployment.
Svelte 5 runes ($state, $derived, $effect) replace stores for reactivity. SvelteKit: routes in src/routes/, +page.svelte / +page.server.ts split, form actions, hooks.server.ts for middleware.
Angular 17+ uses standalone components by default (no NgModule). Signals replace zone.js change detection. Control flow @if/@for/@switch in templates. inject() function over constructor injection.
NestJS: decorator-driven (@Controller, @Module, @Injectable, @UseGuards). Fastify adapter is faster than Express. ValidationPipe + class-validator for DTOs. Dependency injection mirrors Angular.
Hono: ultra-fast TS HTTP, runs on Bun/Deno/Node/Cloudflare Workers. c.req.json()/c.req.query()/c.json() pattern. Built-in middleware: cors, logger, jwt. Type-safe RPC via hono/client.

Prisma: schema-first ORM. prisma generate after schema changes. Use select/include sparingly (over-fetch). Transactions: prisma.$transaction([op1, op2]). N+1 protection: prisma.user.findMany({include: {posts: true}}).

Drizzle: TS-first SQL builder, no codegen runtime. Schema in TS files via pgTable/mysqlTable. Migrations: drizzle-kit generate + drizzle-kit push. Type-safe queries: db.select().from(users).where(eq(users.id, 1)).

tRPC: end-to-end type-safe RPC. Router on server, client uses inferred types. router({ getUser: publicProcedure.input(z.string()).query(...) }). Pairs naturally with Zod for validation. Subscriptions via WebSockets.

Zod: schema validation. z.object({}).strict() rejects unknown keys; z.object({}).passthrough() allows them. .safeParse(x) returns { success, data | error }. Discriminated union: z.discriminatedUnion('kind', [...]).
Vite: ESM-native dev server + Rollup production build. plugins: array order matters. import.meta.env for env vars (must prefix VITE_ to be exposed). HMR is automatic for React/Vue/Svelte.
Vitest: Vite-powered test runner, Jest-compatible API. expect(x).toEqual(...), describe/it/beforeEach. vi.fn() for mocks. Coverage via @vitest/coverage-v8. Snapshot tests with .toMatchSnapshot().

Playwright E2E: page.goto/click/fill, expect(page).toHaveText(). Auto-wait built in (no sleep). Browsers: chromium/firefox/webkit. Codegen: npx playwright codegen URL. Trace viewer for debugging: --trace on.

Node 22 LTS: native TypeScript via --experimental-strip-types (will be default), built-in test runner (node --test), node:fs/promises preferred over fs.promises, fetch is global, --watch for dev hot-reload.
Bun 1.1: drop-in Node replacement, much faster startup. bun install / bun run / bun test. Bun.serve() built-in HTTP. Built-in SQLite via bun:sqlite. import.meta.dir replaces __dirname.

Flask 3: app = Flask(__name__), @app.route('/path', methods=['POST']). request.get_json(), jsonify(data). Use flask-blueprint for modular routes. Production: gunicorn (sync) or hypercorn (async). Avoid mixing blocking + async views.

SQLAlchemy 2.0: Mapped[Type] type annotations replace Column. session.execute(select(User).where(User.id==1)). Async via AsyncSession. relationship(lazy='joined'|'select'|'noload'). Don't expose ORM models in API; use Pydantic DTO.

Pydantic v2 (rewrite in Rust, 5-50x faster). BaseModel.model_validate / model_dump (not parse_obj/dict). field_validator decorator. ConfigDict(from_attributes=True) for ORM mode. Don't use v1 patterns; v1 → v2 is breaking.

pytest 8: fixtures via @pytest.fixture, parametrize via @pytest.mark.parametrize. conftest.py for shared fixtures. assert == is preferred (introspection auto-rewrites). Don't use unittest.TestCase classes — slower + harder to compose.

Rails 7+: import maps for JS (no webpack), Hotwire (Turbo + Stimulus) for SPA-like UX without React. Modern: ActiveRecord encryption, async queries (.load_async). Avoid ActiveRecord callbacks for business logic; use service objects.

Spring Boot 3 requires Java 17+. @RestController + @GetMapping/@PostMapping. Constructor injection over @Autowired. application.yml > properties for config. Spring Data JPA: extend JpaRepository<Entity,ID>. Tests: @SpringBootTest (slow) vs @WebMvcTest (focused).

Phoenix LiveView: server-rendered, websocket-driven UIs without writing JS. mount/3 + handle_event/3 lifecycle. assigns drives the template. Streams (handle_stream) for large lists. Don't put business logic in controllers; use contexts.

Laravel 11: streamlined skeleton (no app/Http/Middleware), routes/web.php + routes/api.php. Eloquent ORM, Blade templates, Livewire/Inertia for SPA-style. php artisan make:* generators. Don't put logic in controllers; use form requests + service classes.

.NET 8 minimal APIs: app.MapGet('/path', handler) over [Controller]. EF Core for ORM (DbContext, DbSet). Records for immutable DTOs. async Task<T> ubiquitous. dotnet user-secrets for local dev secrets. Avoid sync-over-async (.Result, .Wait()).

Tokio: async runtime. #[tokio::main] for main, tokio::spawn for tasks, tokio::select! for racing futures. tokio::sync (Mutex, RwLock, mpsc, broadcast, oneshot). Don't block in async context — use tokio::task::spawn_blocking. tokio::time::sleep, not std::thread::sleep.

Axum: typed extractors (Json<T>, Path<T>, Query<T>, State<T>). Router::new().route('/x', get(handler)). Tower middleware: .layer(TraceLayer::new()). Errors: implement IntoResponse for custom errors. Pairs with sqlx for DB.

Actix-web 4: HttpServer::new(|| App::new()). Handler fn signatures inferred (web::Json, web::Path). web::Data<T> for shared state. Middleware via wrap(). actix-rt is the runtime; can interop with Tokio via actix-rt::Runtime.

SQLx: compile-time-checked SQL. query!("SELECT id FROM users WHERE id = $1", id) — verifies against live DB. PgPool / MySqlPool / SqlitePool. Migrations: sqlx-cli (sqlx migrate add/run/revert). DATABASE_URL must be set at compile time for query! macro.
Diesel: macro-heavy compile-time-safe ORM. diesel migration generate/run. Trade-off: more boilerplate than SQLx but full type-checking of joins/filters.

Serde: derive Serialize/Deserialize on types. serde_json::to_string/from_str. Custom field renames: #[serde(rename = "camelCase")]. Skip on null: #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]. Untagged enums for flexible JSON shapes.

Gin: r := gin.Default(); r.GET('/x', handler). c.JSON(200, gin.H{}). c.ShouldBindJSON(&dto) for validation. Middleware: r.Use(authMiddleware). Production: don't use gin.Default() (debug logger); use gin.New() + explicit middleware.

Echo: alternative Go HTTP framework. e := echo.New(); e.GET('/x', handler). c.JSON(200, data). Validator integration via go-playground/validator. Generally faster than Gin in benchmarks; smaller community.
GORM: struct tag-driven. db.Find(&users). gorm.Model embedded for ID/CreatedAt/UpdatedAt/DeletedAt. Don't use gorm for complex joins; drop to raw SQL.
sqlc: writes SQL, generates Go. Type-safe without ORM overhead. CREATE TABLE in schema.sql, queries in queries.sql, sqlc generate produces typed Go fns. Pairs with pgx for Postgres.
Cobra: Go CLI framework (used by kubectl, hugo, gh). cobra.Command{Use, Short, Run}. Subcommands via AddCommand. Flags: cmd.Flags().StringP('name', 'n', '', 'desc').

Redis 7: SET/GET/INCR/EXPIRE basics. Sorted sets (ZADD/ZRANGE) for leaderboards. Streams (XADD/XREADGROUP) for log/queue patterns. Pub/Sub for fanout. Use Redis Cluster for HA + sharding. Set sensible maxmemory + eviction policy (allkeys-lru typical).

MongoDB 7: documents in collections; flexible schema is a liability without validation. db.collection.createIndex({field: 1}) — every common query needs an index. Aggregation pipeline ($match/$lookup/$group). Don't use $lookup as a frequent join; pre-denormalize.

SQLite: file-based, embedded. Use WAL mode (PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL) for concurrent readers. Foreign keys OFF by default — turn on per-connection. Better-sqlite3 (Node) is sync; libsql/turso adds replication.

ClickHouse: columnar OLAP. ENGINE = MergeTree() PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(date) ORDER BY (key1, key2). Inserts batched (1k+ rows). Don't use POINT lookups; designed for aggregate scans. Materialized views for pre-aggregation.

Kafka: topics partitioned for parallelism. Producer key determines partition. Consumer groups distribute partitions. acks=all + idempotent producer for exactly-once. Schema Registry (Confluent) for Avro/Protobuf evolution. Don't use Kafka as a queue with random consumers.

Docker: multi-stage builds (FROM ... AS builder + FROM scratch/alpine for final). .dockerignore parallels .gitignore. COPY with --chown=. Don't run as root in containers (USER directive). Layer ordering: copy package.json + install BEFORE copying source.

K8s 1.30: Deployments + Services + Ingress. Liveness vs readiness probes (different purposes). resources.requests + limits — don't omit. ConfigMap for non-secrets, Secret for secrets (base64 ≠ encryption; use sealed-secrets). HorizontalPodAutoscaler v2 with custom metrics.

Terraform 1.x: lock provider versions (~> 5.0). State in remote backend (S3 + DynamoDB lock, or Terraform Cloud). Modules: source = './modules/x'. Use for_each over count for stable resource addresses. terraform plan must be reviewed before apply in CI.

GitHub Actions: pin actions to SHA, not tag (supply chain risk). Cache: actions/cache@v4 with restore-keys. Concurrency cancellation: concurrency.cancel-in-progress. Matrix builds via strategy.matrix. Don't put secrets in env at job level; use environment-scoped secrets.
Datadog: APM via dd-trace SDK (auto-instrumentation in JS/Python/Java/Go). RUM for browser. logs.format = JSON for parsing. Tag everything with env+service+version.
Sentry: capture errors + performance. Sentry.init({dsn, tracesSampleRate}). Don't ship dev errors to prod project — environment tag. PII scrubbing via beforeSend.

Prometheus: pull-based, scrapes /metrics. Counter (monotonically increasing), Gauge (up/down), Histogram (bucketed), Summary (quantiles). Don't use Summary for cross-instance percentiles — use Histogram + histogram_quantile.

React Native 0.74+: New Architecture (Fabric + TurboModules) is opt-in but recommended. React Navigation 6: createNativeStackNavigator. Use FlatList (not ScrollView) for long lists. Don't bundle web React libs into RN — they often break native.

Expo SDK 51+: managed workflow handles native config. expo-router for file-based routing. EAS Build for cloud-built native binaries. Expo Modules API for custom native code without ejecting. Don't use deprecated 'unimodules'; migrate to expo-modules.
Flutter 3.22: Impeller renderer default on iOS. Material 3 (useMaterial3: true). Riverpod or Provider for state. Don't rebuild huge widget trees — use const constructors + ValueListenableBuilder.

OpenAI SDK: const client = new OpenAI({apiKey}). chat.completions.create({model, messages}). Function calling: tools array. Streaming via stream:true + for await. Vision via image_url in content array.

Anthropic SDK: const client = new Anthropic(); messages.create({model, max_tokens, messages}). Tool use via tools array. System prompt as top-level field (not in messages). Streaming via .stream() for type-safe events.
LangChain: chain abstractions over LLM calls. LCEL (LangChain Expression Language) for composition. Don't use deprecated AgentExecutor; use langgraph for orchestration.

HuggingFace transformers: AutoModel.from_pretrained(model_id), AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained. Pipeline API for quick prototypes. PEFT/TRL for fine-tuning. Don't load models in worker init; use lazy + cached.

PyTorch 2.x: torch.compile() for 30%+ speedups (no code change usually). Mixed precision via torch.cuda.amp.autocast. Don't .item() in hot loops (CPU↔GPU sync). Use DataLoader with num_workers > 0 + pin_memory=True.
shadcn/ui: not a library — copy-paste pattern. Components live in your repo, you own them. Built on Radix primitives + Tailwind. cn() utility merges tailwind classes safely (clsx + tailwind-merge).
Radix: unstyled, accessible primitives. Dialog/Popover/Select/Tooltip etc. Compose: Trigger + Content + Portal patterns. Don't fight Radix's controlled/uncontrolled modes.

TanStack Query v5: useQuery({queryKey, queryFn}). queryKey is the cache key. Mutations via useMutation({mutationFn, onSuccess}). queryClient.invalidateQueries on success. Don't useEffect to refetch — use staleTime + refetchOnWindowFocus.
Zustand: const useStore = create<State>((set) => ({...})). Slices pattern for large stores. Persist middleware via zustand/middleware. Use shallow comparison in selector for object-returning selects.

Redux Toolkit (RTK): createSlice replaces hand-written reducers. RTK Query for data fetching (alternative to TanStack Query). Don't use the old createStore; configureStore handles middleware + devtools.
Auth0: identity-as-a-service. Login: /authorize redirect or embedded React SDK. Validate access tokens server-side via JWKS. Rules and Actions for custom claims; prefer Actions (Rules deprecated).

Clerk: opinionated auth UI + backend. <ClerkProvider> wraps app. useUser/useAuth hooks. Server-side: auth() in Next.js server components. Webhooks for user.created/updated events. Pricing scales with MAU.

Supabase: hosted Postgres + Auth + Storage + Realtime + Edge Functions. Row-Level Security (RLS) MUST be enabled — public-by-default is the gotcha. Auth: signInWithOAuth/signInWithPassword. Realtime via supabase.channel(...).on('postgres_changes', ...).

Keycloak: open-source identity broker. Realm = tenant. Clients (apps) request auth via OIDC/SAML. Admin console at /auth/admin. Use distroless image in prod; bundle theme + realm config via init container.
esbuild: 10-100x faster than webpack/Rollup. Limited plugin ecosystem vs Rollup. Good for libraries, server bundles, dev. For complex SPAs use Vite (esbuild for dev, Rollup for prod).
Webpack 5: legacy choice; Vite/Turbopack are faster. Module Federation for micro-frontends. Tree-shaking requires sideEffects: false in package.json. Prefer Vite/Bun/esbuild for new projects.
SWC: Rust-based TS/JS transformer. Used by Next.js, Vite (alternative to esbuild), Vitest. Custom plugins via Rust crate. .swcrc for config.
Rollup: for libraries. ESM-first. Plugin ecosystem (commonjs, node-resolve, typescript). Vite uses Rollup for production builds.
Celery: Python task queue. Redis or RabbitMQ broker. @app.task decorator. Don't put model instances in task args (pickle issues); pass IDs and refetch.

RabbitMQ: AMQP broker. Direct/fanout/topic exchanges. Durable queues + persistent messages survive restarts. Use prefetch_count to avoid worker overload. Dead-letter exchange for failed-message handling.

Elasticsearch 8: secure-by-default (TLS + auth). Index templates + ILM for time-series. _bulk endpoint for high-throughput ingest. Aggregations: terms/date_histogram/percentiles. Scroll API deprecated — use search_after for pagination.

GraphQL: schema-first or code-first. DataLoader for N+1 batching. Persisted queries for prod (security + caching). Apollo Server, Yoga, or Pothos (TS-first). Avoid deeply-nested queries — depth limits in production.
Hasura: GraphQL over Postgres. Permissions defined declaratively per role. Actions for custom logic (HTTP webhook). Don't expose Hasura console publicly; lock to admin.

Stripe: server-side SDK + Stripe.js for client. Payment Intents API > legacy Charges. Webhook signature verification (Stripe-Signature header). Test cards: 4242424242424242. Webhook idempotency via event.id deduplication.
Twilio: SMS/Voice/Email. messages.create({to, from, body}). E.164 phone format ('+1...'). Webhook validation via X-Twilio-Signature. Subaccounts for multi-tenant billing isolation.
Playwright Test: test('name', async ({page}) => {}). Auto-waits for elements. trace: 'on-first-retry' for debugging flaky tests. testMatch in playwright.config for pattern selection.
Jest: legacy choice for new TS projects (Vitest is faster + Vite-native). describe/it/expect. Mocks: jest.fn(), jest.mock(). Use --testPathPattern for selective runs.
Cypress: alternative E2E framework. Runs in browser context (not over devtools-protocol like Playwright). Custom commands via Cypress.Commands.add. Cypress Cloud for parallel runs + flake detection.

Storybook 8: Component Story Format 3 (CSF3). Vite or Webpack builder. Test components in isolation. Chromatic for visual regression. Don't write business logic in stories — keep them about display/state.
TanStack Router: type-safe routing. File-based routing. Loader + action pattern. Search params are first-class typed. Alternative to React Router for new projects.

SolidJS: signals-based reactivity, no virtual DOM. createSignal/createMemo/createEffect. JSX compiles to direct DOM ops. Faster than React for many workloads. SolidStart for SSR. Don't use React patterns (useState etc.) — different mental model.

Qwik: resumability over hydration. component$() / useSignal() / useTask$(). Code-splits at function-call boundaries automatically. Fast initial load (no JS until interaction). Trade-off: harder mental model, smaller ecosystem.

htmx: HTML-over-the-wire. hx-get/hx-post return HTML fragments, swapped into the DOM. Server renders all HTML; minimal JS. Pairs with Hyperscript or Alpine for client logic. Underrated for CRUD apps + SSR-friendly stacks.
Alpine.js: 'jQuery for the modern web'. x-data, x-on:click, x-model. Minimal — for sprinkling interactivity on server-rendered HTML. Alternative to writing vanilla JS for forms/dropdowns/modals.

Deno 2: Node.js-compatible (npm: imports work). Built-in TS, no tsconfig needed for app code. Permissions explicit (--allow-read, --allow-net). Deno.serve() built-in. deno fmt / deno lint / deno test included. Fresh framework for SSR.
Rocket: ergonomic Rust HTTP framework. #[get("/path")] / #[post("/path")] decorators. Type-safe form parsing. Slightly slower than Axum/Actix but easier API. State managed via &State<T>.
Ktor: Kotlin asynchronous HTTP. embeddedServer(Netty, port = 8080) { routing { get("/") {...} } }. Built on coroutines. Plugins for auth, JSON, sessions. Pairs with Exposed (Kotlin SQL DSL) for DB.

SwiftUI: declarative iOS/macOS. @State (local), @Binding (passed-down), @StateObject (created here), @ObservedObject (passed in), @EnvironmentObject (ambient). VStack/HStack/ZStack for layout. Use Observation framework (iOS 17+) over ObservableObject when possible.

Jetpack Compose: declarative Android UI. @Composable functions, remember { mutableStateOf(0) } for state. LaunchedEffect for side effects. CompositionLocal for ambient values. Material 3 components. Don't recompose entire trees — use derivedStateOf + key().

Biome: Rust-based formatter + linter (Prettier + ESLint replacement). 30x faster. biome.json config. biome format / biome lint / biome check. Drop-in for many projects but strict about config — won't auto-create biome.json.

Ruff: Rust-based Python linter + formatter. 100x faster than flake8 + isort + black combined. ruff check / ruff format. Configure in pyproject.toml [tool.ruff]. Replaces flake8, isort, pyupgrade, pep8-naming, autoflake all at once.

Turborepo 2: monorepo task runner. turbo.json defines task pipeline (build, test, lint dependencies). Remote caching via Vercel or self-hosted. Filter: turbo run build --filter=app-name. Pairs with pnpm workspaces or yarn berry.
Nx: monorepo + plugin-driven scaffolding. nx.json + project.json per workspace. nx serve / nx test. Plugins for Next/Angular/Vue/Express. Affected commands: nx affected -t test --base=main.

Pulumi: IaC in real programming languages (TS, Python, Go, .NET). new aws.s3.Bucket('name', {...}). Stacks (dev/prod). State in Pulumi Cloud or self-hosted backend. Differs from Terraform: imperative composition, not HCL.

Astro 5: content-first framework with islands architecture (zero JS by default; opt-in interactive components via client:load/client:idle/client:visible). MDX-native, SSG by default, SSR via adapters. Component frameworks (React/Vue/Svelte/Solid) interop via integrations. Good fit: blogs, docs sites, marketing pages.

Stencil: web-component compiler. @Component({tag, styleUrl}) decorator, JSX templates, TypeScript. Output: framework-agnostic Custom Elements that work in React/Angular/Vue/vanilla. Owned by Ionic; used for design systems shipped across teams with different stacks.

Refine: React framework for internal tools / admin panels / dashboards. Headless by default — bring your own UI (Ant, MUI, Chakra). Built-in CRUD hooks (useTable/useForm/useShow) wired to data providers (REST/GraphQL/Supabase/Hasura). Auth + access-control + i18n + audit log primitives.

AngularJS 1.x — legacy, end-of-life since 2022. ng-controller, ng-model, ng-repeat, $scope. NOT the same as modern Angular (2+, TypeScript). Migration paths: ngUpgrade for incremental, or rewrite. Don't start new projects on AngularJS; flag as tech debt.

Falcon 4: minimalist Python WSGI/ASGI framework, optimized for raw speed and APIs. Resource classes with on_get / on_post / on_put / on_delete methods. No magic — explicit middleware chain. Faster than Flask + FastAPI for simple JSON APIs at the cost of fewer batteries.

Bottle: single-file Python micro-framework (no deps beyond stdlib). @route('/path') decorator. Use for prototypes, internal tools, embedded admin endpoints. Don't use for production APIs at scale; FastAPI/Flask have better ecosystems.

CherryPy: object-oriented Python framework — class methods become URL handlers. @cherrypy.expose decorator. Built-in HTTP server. Mature (since 2002) but small community; consider FastAPI/Flask for new work.
TurboGears: full-stack Python (SQLAlchemy + Genshi/Kajiki + Pylons-derived routing). @expose() returns dict, template renders. Niche compared to Django/Flask; only encounter in legacy projects.

Hapi.js: configuration-centric Node.js framework. server.route({method, path, handler, options}). Built-in input validation (Joi), authentication strategies, caching, plugins. More verbose than Express; better defaults for security + structure.

LoopBack 4: API-first framework from IBM. Decorator-driven (@model, @repository, @get, @post). OpenAPI-first design — generates client SDKs. Heavyweight; chosen for enterprise REST APIs that need codegen + RBAC + connectors to legacy systems.

Nitro: server toolkit powering Nuxt 3 server routes; usable standalone. defineEventHandler() in /server/api/. Adapters: node-server, vercel, cloudflare-pages, deno-deploy, aws-lambda. Universal output target — write once, deploy anywhere.

Symfony 7: component-based PHP framework. Doctrine ORM, Twig templates, Symfony Console for CLI. Annotation/attribute routing (#[Route]). Reusable components — Laravel uses many of them under the hood. Configure via YAML/PHP. Strong DDD + hexagonal-architecture culture.
Laminas (formerly Zend Framework): enterprise PHP framework. Module-based, very explicit configuration. Heavyweight; mostly seen in legacy enterprise PHP. New projects: prefer Symfony or Laravel.

CakePHP 5: convention-over-configuration PHP. Bake CLI for scaffolding. ORM with associations, behaviors, query builder. Smaller community than Laravel/Symfony but stable and docs-heavy. Good fit for medium CRUD apps.

Yii 3: high-performance PHP. Active Record ORM, Gii code generator. Yii 2 still widely deployed; Yii 3 is the modern rewrite (PSR-compliant, packages-as-modules). Used in Asia-Pacific markets and CRM products.

Slim 4: PHP micro-framework for APIs. PSR-7 + PSR-15 native (interoperable middleware). Bring your own DI container (PHP-DI common), template engine, ORM. Ideal for thin REST services without Laravel/Symfony overhead.
FuelPHP: HMVC-architected PHP. Smaller community; mostly maintenance mode. New projects: prefer Laravel or Symfony.

Sinatra: minimalist Ruby DSL. get '/' do ... end. No conventions — you build the structure. Perfect for tiny APIs, embeddable admin endpoints, glue scripts. Pair with Sequel or ActiveRecord for DB; Rack for middleware.

Spring Framework 6 (separate from Spring Boot): the underlying DI + AOP + transaction core. @Component/@Service/@Repository/@Configuration. Boot bundles it with auto-config + embedded server. Spring Framework alone is for non-Boot projects (rare in 2026).

Play Framework 3: reactive Scala/Java web framework. routes file maps URLs to controller methods. Akka HTTP under the hood. Compile-time dependency injection (Macwire/Guice). Niche compared to Spring Boot; mostly used in Scala shops.

Apache Struts 6: legacy Java MVC. Action classes + struts.xml routing. Mostly seen maintaining old apps; new Java projects use Spring Boot. CVE history (Equifax breach was Struts) — keep current with patches if you have it.
Beego: full-featured Go MVC. ORM, cache, session, monitoring built in. Heavier than Gin/Echo; provides bee CLI for scaffolding. Popular in China; smaller community elsewhere.

Chi: lightweight idiomatic Go HTTP router. r.Get('/x', handler) over standard net/http; pure stdlib types (no custom Context). Composable middleware. Good fit for projects that want control + stdlib alignment over Gin's framework feel.

Vapor 4: server-side Swift. app.get('hello') { req in 'Hello' }. Async/await native. Fluent ORM (Postgres/MySQL/SQLite/Mongo). EventLoopFuture for legacy code. Swift NIO for performance. Good fit when iOS team wants to share Swift code on the backend.

Ionic 8: hybrid mobile via web tech (React/Angular/Vue). <ion-* /> components. Capacitor as the native runtime (replaced Cordova). iOS + Android + PWA from one codebase. Trade-off: not native-feeling for complex UI; great for forms-heavy line-of-business apps.

Xamarin: legacy Microsoft cross-platform. Replaced by .NET MAUI in 2024 (Xamarin support ended May 2024). Don't start new projects on Xamarin; migrate to MAUI. Existing Xamarin apps: support window has closed for new Microsoft updates.

Capacitor 6: web-to-native bridge from the Ionic team. Replaces Cordova. Plugins for camera/filesystem/geolocation as ESM imports. Pair with any web framework (React/Vue/Svelte/vanilla). npm install @capacitor/cli + npx cap sync.

Payload 3: TypeScript headless CMS, Postgres or MongoDB. Code-first content modeling (collections + globals + fields). Auto-generated REST + GraphQL APIs. Built-in auth, access control, file uploads (S3/Cloudinary), versioning, drafts. Self-hostable; integrates as a Next.js plugin.

Strapi 5: open-source headless CMS. Admin UI for content modeling (no-code-friendly). REST + GraphQL. Postgres/MySQL/SQLite. Plugins for auth providers, i18n, media. v5 introduced doc service + new content API. Self-host or Strapi Cloud.

AdonisJS 6: full-stack TypeScript framework, Laravel-inspired conventions. Lucid ORM, Edge templates, built-in auth + validator + mailer + queue. node ace make:* scaffolding. More opinionated than NestJS; less popular but very batteries-included.
Sapper: deprecated. Predecessor to SvelteKit (since 2021). Migrate to SvelteKit for new work. Existing Sapper apps: migration guide on svelte.dev; routing + endpoints differ.

TensorFlow 2.16: Keras 3 is the default high-level API. tf.data for input pipelines, tf.GradientTape for custom training loops. SavedModel format for deployment. TF Lite for mobile/embedded. Don't use TF1.x patterns (sessions, placeholders) — they're deprecated.
Apache MXNet: deep learning framework, mostly maintenance mode after Amazon refocused on PyTorch. Niche; new projects use PyTorch or JAX.

Keras 3: multi-backend (TensorFlow, JAX, PyTorch) high-level neural net API. keras.Sequential or keras.Model subclass. model.fit(x, y, epochs=10). Use keras.ops for backend-agnostic ops. Great for prototyping; for production training prefer the underlying framework.

scikit-learn 1.5: classical ML (not deep learning). fit/predict API, Pipeline + ColumnTransformer for preprocessing, GridSearchCV for hyperparameter search. Use with pandas DataFrames. Don't use for very-large datasets — switch to dask-ml or distributed alternatives at >10M rows.

Mocha: feature-rich JS test framework. describe/it/before/after. Bring your own assertion lib (chai common). Plugins for parallel runs, coverage. Vitest is faster + Vite-native; Mocha for legacy projects or non-Vite stacks.

JUnit 5 (Jupiter): @Test on methods, @BeforeAll/@BeforeEach lifecycle. @ParameterizedTest with @ValueSource/@CsvSource. Assertions: org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions or AssertJ. Mockito for mocking. JUnit 4 still widely deployed; new projects use 5.

Selenium 4: cross-browser automation. WebDriver protocol. Slower than Playwright/Cypress; primary value is the wide language binding (Python/Java/C#/Ruby/JS). Use Selenium Grid for parallel cross-browser. For new JS projects, prefer Playwright.

Unity 6: dominant cross-platform game engine. C# scripts attach to GameObjects. Update() / FixedUpdate() / LateUpdate() lifecycle. UI Toolkit (USS/UXML) replaces UGUI. DOTS (Burst + ECS) for performance-critical. Asset Store + Package Manager for plugins.

Unreal Engine 5: AAA game development. C++ for systems, Blueprint visual scripting for designers. UCLASS/UPROPERTY/UFUNCTION decorators wire to the reflection system. Lumen + Nanite for next-gen graphics. UE5 is free until $1M revenue, then 5% royalty.

Godot 4.3: open-source game engine. GDScript (Python-like) or C# bindings. Scene = tree of nodes. signal-based event system. Lightweight (engine ~100MB). Strong 2D, improving 3D. Good for indie + jam projects; community + docs growing fast.

Phaser 3: HTML5 2D game framework. Scene-based architecture (preload/create/update). Built-in physics (Arcade, Matter). Sprites, tilemaps, animations. Distributes as web canvas/WebGL. Not for 3D; use Three.js / Babylon for that.
Meteor 3: full-stack JS, real-time DB sync via DDP. MongoDB-first. Originally pioneered isomorphic JS; now niche. New projects: usually prefer Next/Nuxt/SvelteKit + tRPC for type-safety.

Ember.js 5: opinionated framework with strong conventions. Octane edition uses Glimmer + auto-tracking. ember-cli for scaffolding. Smaller mindshare than React/Vue but very stable; popular in long-lived enterprise apps (LinkedIn, Heroku Dashboard).
Backbone.js: legacy thin MVC for jQuery era. Models + Collections + Views over a REST API. Mostly maintenance mode. Existing apps: viable if working; new projects prefer modern frameworks.

Koa 2: Express's spiritual successor (same authors). async/await-native middleware (no callback hell). ctx (request + response merged) replaces Express's req/res. No built-in router; use @koa/router. Smaller ecosystem than Express; cleaner for new TS code.
Sails.js: convention-over-config Node MVC, Rails-inspired. Waterline ORM (multi-DB adapter). Auto-generated REST API from blueprints. Niche today; new TS projects use NestJS or AdonisJS.

Blazor (.NET 8+): C# in the browser via WebAssembly OR server-rendered (SignalR streams DOM diffs). Razor syntax (@page, @code). Component model similar to Svelte. Pros: full C# stack, type-safe end-to-end. Cons: WASM bundle large; SSR limits offline.

ASP.NET Core (.NET 8): cross-platform Microsoft web framework. Minimal APIs (app.MapGet) or MVC (Controllers). EF Core for ORM. Identity for auth. Razor Pages for server-rendered UI; Blazor for SPA. Strong perf benchmarks; mature observability.

Quarkus 3: Kubernetes-native Java. Live coding, fast startup (GraalVM native image). Microprofile-based REST + reactive (Mutiny). Compiled to native binary: subsecond startup, low memory — ideal for serverless / containers.

Micronaut 4: JVM microservices framework. Compile-time DI (no reflection at runtime → fast startup, native-friendly). @Controller / @Get / @Post. Good Java + Kotlin + Groovy support. Competes with Quarkus for the cloud-native Java niche.

Vert.x 4: reactive toolkit for the JVM (Java/Kotlin/Scala). Event-loop + verticles model. Polyglot — call across modules in different languages. Used for high-throughput APIs, IoT gateways, WebSocket fan-out. Steeper learning curve than Spring.

Akka: actor-based concurrency on JVM (Scala/Java). ActorSystem + Behaviors. Akka Streams for backpressure-aware data pipelines. Akka HTTP for the web layer. License changed (BSL 1.1) in 2022 — paid above $25M revenue. Pekko is the Apache fork if you need Apache 2.0.

gRPC: protobuf-defined services over HTTP/2. .proto schema + codegen for client/server in many languages. Streaming (unary, server-stream, client-stream, bidi). Use grpc-web for browsers (limited). Pair with Buf for schema linting + breaking-change detection.

Apollo: GraphQL ecosystem. Apollo Server 4 (standalone server). Apollo Client 3 (React hooks for queries/mutations/subscriptions). Apollo Federation for stitched microservice schemas. Apollo Studio for schema registry + monitoring. Heavy compared to Yoga/Pothos; full feature set.

Relay: Meta's GraphQL client. Strong patterns: fragments colocated with components, automatic data deduping, store normalization. Compiler step (relay-compiler) generates types. More opinionated than Apollo; pays off in large React apps.
